articles.title: Signed and sealed: US-Iran war-ending deal takes effect two days early ahead of Burgenstock ceremony
articles.summary: The US and Iran's memorandum of understanding ending their war entered into force two days ahead of schedule, after Trump signed the text at a candlelit dinner outside Paris and Iranian President Masoud Pezeshkian signed remotely. The 14-point accord commits Iran to not develop nuclear weapons, ends the war 'on all fronts,' reopens the Strait of Hormuz, and references a proposed $300 billion reconstruction fund. A formal ceremony is still set for Friday, June 19, at the Burgenstock resort near Lucerne, with Switzerland facilitating and Pakistan and Qatar credited as mediators.

articles.title: Israeli air attacks on southern Lebanon kill three despite US-Iran deal
articles.summary: Israeli strikes on southern Lebanon killed at least three people a day after the US-Iran agreement called for an end to the war on all fronts, including Lebanon. Lebanon's National News Agency said an Israeli drone hit a car near Kfar Tebnit, killing two, while at least one person was killed in a separate strike in Zabadin. Hezbollah's parliamentary bloc head Mohammad Raad said Israel's campaign to crush the group had 'failed.' The continued fire underscores Lebanon as the main flashpoint testing the new accord.

articles.title: Israel negotiating with US to keep troops in southern Lebanon, refuses to retreat from Litani
articles.summary: Israeli officials said they are holding 'stubborn negotiations' with Washington to maintain a military presence in southern Lebanon despite the new US-Iran agreement, with one senior official saying troops would not pull back south of the Litani River. Iran has warned that any Israeli forces remaining in Lebanon, or any further strikes there, would constitute a violation of the deal. Israeli officials countered that 'Trump's agreement does not bind us,' setting up the occupation question as the central post-signing dispute.

articles.title: Poll: most Israelis say Netanyahu's conduct hurt Israel in Iran deal; 71% distrust Trump
articles.summary: New surveys show deep Israeli unease over the US-Iran agreement: a poll found 71% of Israelis do not trust Trump to look out for their interests, just 11% feel Israel won the war, and a majority believe Prime Minister Netanyahu's conduct damaged Israel's position. A Maariv poll put Likud down three seats to 22, with Gadi Eisenkot's Yashar party pulling even at 22 and Eisenkot leading Netanyahu 44%-40% in a head-to-head. The numbers point to mounting domestic political fallout as the deal is finalized.

articles.title: What the Trump-Iran 14-point plan says about Lebanon, Hormuz and uranium
articles.summary: An analysis of the leaked 14-point memorandum lays out its core terms: Iran reaffirms it 'shall not develop nuclear weapons' and accepts constraints on uranium enrichment, the Strait of Hormuz is to be reopened to commercial shipping, and the war ends across all fronts. The text's treatment of Lebanon is ambiguous on the timing of any Israeli withdrawal, leaving room for the dispute now playing out. The plan also references sanctions relief and a reconstruction fund for Iran.

articles.title: Gulf states wary of funding $300bn Iran reconstruction, fear it would empower a rival
articles.summary: Gulf capitals from Riyadh to Doha remain skeptical of the US-Iran deal and hesitant to bankroll the proposed $300 billion reconstruction fund, worried that freeing up Iranian resources would let Tehran reinvest in militias in Iraq, Syria, Lebanon and Yemen that threaten Gulf security. Saudi-owned outlets stressed the need for Iran to dismantle its proxies for durable containment, not just a ceasefire, and warned of Iranian threats to Gulf desalination and power infrastructure.

articles.title: Three Palestinians killed in Gaza strike as West Bank operations and settler attacks continue
articles.summary: Three Palestinians were killed and several wounded in an Israeli airstrike on a vehicle in Gaza City, while Israeli military operations and settler violence persisted across the occupied West Bank. Rights monitors said Palestinians continue to face killings, forced displacement and attacks on places of worship, and the EU sanctioned individual settlers and outpost-building organizations. The Gaza Health Ministry says more than 1,000 Palestinians have been killed since the October 2025 ceasefire took effect.

articles.title: Oil prices fall and dollar steadies as US-Iran deal eases supply concerns
articles.summary: Crude prices slid and the dollar held steady as markets digested the interim US-Iran agreement and the prospect of the Strait of Hormuz reopening. A handful of ships have begun moving back toward the Gulf and Iranian tankers are loading crude, the first tentative signs of a restart after months of de facto blockade, though analysts caution that returning traffic to prewar levels could take weeks and full mine-clearing up to six months.

articles.title: US denied Israel's request to review Iran deal text before signing
articles.summary: Israeli officials said Washington declined Israel's request to view the full text of the US-Iran memorandum prior to the signing, fueling Israeli anxieties that its interests were sidelined in the final accord. The episode added to friction between Trump and Netanyahu after Trump publicly rebuked the Israeli prime minister at the G7 over the Lebanon strikes, and feeds the broader Israeli narrative that the country emerged weakened from the war.

articles.title: Houthis maintain ban on Israeli-linked ships in Red Sea as deal takes hold
articles.summary: Yemen's Houthis kept in place their declared ban on Israeli-linked maritime movement through the Red Sea even as the US-Iran ceasefire took effect, after firing missiles toward the Tel Aviv area earlier in the week. Shipping monitors reported a structured pause under tension, with some container carriers cautiously restarting Red Sea services while the group ties any future maritime action to Israel's conduct in Lebanon and Gaza.
